description:
You are a database reliability engineer who has just logged in to a brand-new Linux host.  
Your goal is to prepare a very small local “backup environment” that other automation will later use.  
Complete ALL of the following requirements from the command line.  No sudo/root access is necessary.

1. Create the directory  
   /home/user/db_backups  

2. Inside that directory create an executable shell script named  
   /home/user/db_backups/pg_backup.sh  
   The script’s contents must be **exactly** the 4 lines shown below (no extra spaces or blank lines).  Make sure the first line is a she-bang and that every line ends with a UNIX newline character “\n”.

   ```
   #!/bin/bash
   # Simple PostgreSQL backup script
   TIMESTAMP=$(date +"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
   pg_dumpall -U postgres > "$PG_BACKUP_DIR/postgres_backup_$TIMESTAMP.sql"
   ```

3. Append the single line shown below to the end of the file /home/user/.bashrc.  
   Do NOT remove or alter anything already present in that file; just add this one new line:

   ```
   export PG_BACKUP_DIR=/home/user/db_backups
   ```

4. After you have performed steps 1-3, create a plain-text log file named  
   /home/user/db_backups/backup_setup.log  
   The file must contain **exactly** the four lines, in the precise order, listed here (each followed by a “\n”; no extra spaces or blank lines):

   STATUS: COMPLETE  
   DIRECTORY_CREATED: /home/user/db_backups  
   SCRIPT_CREATED: /home/user/db_backups/pg_backup.sh  
   ENV_ADDED: PG_BACKUP_DIR  

The automated tests will verify:

• The directory and files exist at the exact paths given.  
• /home/user/db_backups/pg_backup.sh is executable and its contents match the 4-line listing verbatim.  
• /home/user/.bashrc ends with the single export line shown.  
• /home/user/db_backups/backup_setup.log matches the 4-line format above character-for-character.

You should perform whatever commands are necessary (mkdir, echo, cat, chmod, etc.) to satisfy all of the checks.
truth:
Expected final state after the agent finishes:

1. Path /home/user/db_backups must exist and be a directory.

2. File /home/user/db_backups/pg_backup.sh  
   • Permissions: executable for user (at least 0700 is acceptable; 0755 is typical).  
   • Exact 4-line content (including newlines at line ends, no trailing blank line):

   Line1: "#!/bin/bash\n"  
   Line2: "# Simple PostgreSQL backup script\n"  
   Line3: "TIMESTAMP=$(date +\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\")\n"  
   Line4: "pg_dumpall -U postgres > \"$PG_BACKUP_DIR/postgres_backup_$TIMESTAMP.sql\"\n"

3. File /home/user/.bashrc ends with the literal text  
   "export PG_BACKUP_DIR=/home/user/db_backups\n"  
   (there may be other content above; the new line must be the final line).

4. Log file /home/user/db_backups/backup_setup.log contains exactly:

   Line1: "STATUS: COMPLETE\n"  
   Line2: "DIRECTORY_CREATED: /home/user/db_backups\n"  
   Line3: "SCRIPT_CREATED: /home/user/db_backups/pg_backup.sh\n"  
   Line4: "ENV_ADDED: PG_BACKUP_DIR\n"

No additional lines or whitespace are allowed in any of the monitored files.
